actions-list.actionItem: The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6480.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Taylor moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 6480 — "To direct the Administrator of General Services to submit a report to Congress on the state of the real estate portfolio of the Public Building Service, and for other purposes."
The title of the measure was amended. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 2474.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Taylor moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 2474 — "To require the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the capability of the Appalachian Regional Commission to include low-orbit satellites in broadband projects, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 4624.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Walberg moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 4624 — "To amend the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996 to establish requirements for unified boxing organizations, to further enhance the well-being of professional boxers, and for other purposes."
The Speaker announced that votes on suspensions, if ordered, will be postponed until a time to be announced.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 24.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches.
The House received a message from the Senate. The Senate passed S. 629, S. 688, S. 843, S. 921, S. 1092, S. 2563, S. 4123, and S. 4164. Senate agreed to S. Con. Res. 29.
The Chair designated Mr. Beyer to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 24.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 2:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 2:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Jeff Hurd to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
